entities.name: Central Retail Corporation Public Company Limited

financial.note: Acts as registrar for the CRC30OA 2026 series; rising interest rates increase the cost of servicing their 124 billion THB debt pool.
Provides working capital and strategic underwriting; debt-to-equity ratios are sensitive to BoT policy rate shifts.

supply_chain.note: Sister company and primary landlord; CRC's retail footprint is anchored in CPN's 44+ shopping malls nationwide.
Vietnam accounts for ~25% of revenue; performance of GO! Malls and GO! Hypermarkets is critical for EBITDA growth.

supply_chain.entity: L'Oréal / Estée Lauder / Luxury Fashion Groups
supply_chain.note: Inventory for the Fashion segment (Central/Robinson) is highly sensitive to consumer discretionary spending and brand availability.

competitors.note: Main rival in the Food/Wholesale sector through Makro and Lotus's; competition for the 'Go Wholesale' segment.
Primary competitor to Thaiwatsadu in the Hardline/Home Improvement market.
Direct competition in the Vietnamese modern trade and grocery sector (Bach Hoa Xanh vs. Tops/Go!).

macro_commodity_factors.logic: Projected 35.5M arrivals in 2026 drive foot traffic and high-ticket sales in tourist-centric department stores.
A weaker THB inflates the cost of imported luxury fashion goods and global retail inventory, though it aids Vietnam translation gains.
Directly correlates with same-store sales growth (SSSG) across Fashion, Hardline, and Food segments.
Affects logistics and electricity costs (Ft) for operating a massive multi-format brick-and-mortar network.
